office_does: The county council adopts budgets and policy for land use, public health, infrastructure, and county services.
race_blurb: The field differs on public investment, housing tools, growth, taxes, homelessness services, and incumbent experience.

candidates.summary: Urban farmer supporting infill housing tools, transit, service funding, environmental protection, and limits on outward growth and data centers.
highlights: Supports land trusts, cottage housing, ADUs, transit, and housing partnerships [S1][S2]
Opposes data centers and urban-growth-boundary expansion [S1]

spending.basis: Supports public transit and funded service and housing partnerships.

housing.basis: Combines infill deregulation with land trusts and nonprofit housing partnerships.

climate.basis: Explicit protection of farmland, water, forests, and green space, though limited energy detail.

experience.basis: Private and community work with no elected record.

candidates.summary: Logistics and business professional supporting mental-health, addiction, and homelessness coordination alongside fiscal accountability.
highlights: Supports service investment and countywide homelessness coordination [S1]

spending.basis: Explicitly combines service expansion with fiscal accountability.

safety.basis: Leans on mental-health and addiction services for safer-neighborhood outcomes.

experience.basis: Business and logistics outsider with no elected experience.

candidates.summary: Incumbent councilor supporting shelter, treatment, affordable-housing preservation, safety, clean water, and senior housing.
highlights: Reports funding a 120-bed shelter with treatment [S1]
Protected mobile-home parks and advanced senior housing [S1]

spending.basis: Uses public investment for shelter, treatment, housing, and environmental projects.

housing.basis: Uses regulatory protection and public investment to preserve affordable and senior housing.

safety.basis: Integrates shelter, addiction, and mental-health treatment with public-safety investment.

climate.basis: Supports clean-water restoration, though detailed energy policy is absent.

experience.basis: Only incumbent with a county legislative record.

candidates.summary: Small-business owner emphasizing tax restraint, responsible budgets, public safety, balanced growth, transportation, and renewable energy.
highlights: Supports a tax-last approach and responsible budgeting [S1]
Supports public safety and affordable reliable renewable energy [S1]

climate.basis: Renewable-energy support is paired with affordability and reliability.

experience.basis: Small-business and community outsider with no elected record.
